---
title: "Session 04 teaching notes: Python 1"
session_id: session-04
event_id: null
---

### Session 4 — Python and pandas basics

TODO: add spyder setup

Python:
- motivation: existing libraries!
TODO: use Anaconda (be prepared to answer questions)
spytertest.py (in canvas) mit studis am Anfang der python session step-by-step durchgehen
use setwd() for spyder

PR : teilt 1_pandas.py (codes für alle Folien)

p.13: abhängig von Zeit: in-session or homework

TODO: check pandas version? 3.0?

Aufgaben: komplett (soweit die Zeit da ist)
Bei Zeitnot: Aufgaben nach Haus geben
Python Lösungen: werden von Tutoren besprochen


## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise               | Summary                                                                                                          | Files                                                                                      | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------- |
|       5 | Basic operators        | Run arithmetic, division, modulo, exponentiation, and string-concatenation expressions.                          | —                                                                                          |                      |
|      11 | Trace program output   | Predict the results of assignments, reassignment, multiple assignment, and arithmetic expressions.               | —                                                                                          |                      |
|      13 | Basic formula programs | Calculate a circle’s area and the future value of an investment using variables defined in the program.          | `CircleArea1.py`<br>`FutureValue.py`                                                       |                      |
|      22 | Import and export data | Load country and insurance data, inspect DataFrame dimensions and columns, and export the insurance data to CSV. | `countries.csv`<br>`countries.py`<br>`insurance.xlsx`<br>`insurance.py`<br>`insurance.csv` |                      |



<!--
    ## Session overview

    - **Focus:** Python
    - **Session title:** Python 1
    - **Coverage target:** Until **Accessing data**

    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->
